epics.title: Telegram Session and Proxy Management
epics.jtbd: When I manage outreach infrastructure, I want to securely connect and cycle multiple Telegram accounts through proxies, so that I can maintain active sessions without triggering automated bans.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce account ban rate from baseline to <5% per campaign
epics.tocConstraintRef: Telegram API rate limits and anti-spam detection

slices.title: Evaluate and Select Telegram Client Core
slices.jtbd: When implementing the Telegram client core for this epic, I want to evaluate TDLib JNI vs external bridges, so that we have a stable and maintainable integration.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the need for Telegram API access, When evaluating libraries, Then a documented architecture decision is made.
Given the decision, When evaluating deployment, Then the required runtime dependencies are defined.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce connection drops to <1% per hour

slices.title: Define Telegram Account and Proxy Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to define the schema for Telegram accounts and proxies, so that the session manager can securely store and retrieve them.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given PostgreSQL is used, When defining the schema, Then tables for accounts, proxies, and session status are created.
Given the tables, When checking constraints, Then one proxy is uniquely assigned to one account.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% referential integrity for accounts and proxies

slices.title: Define Session Management API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing the shared API for this epic, I want to define the endpoints and DTOs for account management, so that frontend and backend can be built in parallel.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the account management requirements, When defining the API, Then endpoints for OTP auth and proxy binding are documented.
Given the API contract, When checked by developers, Then it fully supports frontend UI needs.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce API contract misalignments to 0 during parallel work

slices.title: Implement Session Manager Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want to build the Telegram auth flow and anti-fraud timing logic, so that accounts operate securely.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an OTP or session file, When submitted, Then the backend authenticates the account via the Telegram Core.
Given an active session, When sending a message, Then the system applies random delays and typing status.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Backend session handling and anti-fraud logic
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Maintain 99.9% uptime for active Telegram sessions

slices.title: Implement Account Management Frontend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want to build Svelte interfaces for adding accounts and proxies, so that administrators can manage their pool.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the Svelte application, When an admin visits the accounts page, Then they see a list of accounts and their statuses.
Given the add account form, When submitted with OTP, Then the account is securely linked.

epics.title: Outreach Campaign Management
epics.jtbd: When I run cold outreach, I want to upload lead lists and configure spintax message templates, so that I can automatically distribute unique messages across my account pool.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase messages sent per day by 300% without triggering flood limits

slices.title: Define Campaign and Lead Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to define the schema for campaigns, leads, and templates, so that the campaign engine has structured data to operate on.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given PostgreSQL is used, When defining the schema, Then tables for campaigns, leads, and message templates are created.
Given a campaign, When it runs, Then lead statuses are accurately tracked.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 0% data loss during high-volume campaign execution

slices.title: Define Campaign API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing the shared API for this epic, I want to define the endpoints for CSV import and template creation, so that frontend and backend can be built in parallel.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the need to upload leads, When defining the API, Then a CSV upload endpoint is documented.
Given the campaign configuration, When defining the API, Then endpoints for setting spintax templates are documented.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% coverage of campaign configuration in the API contract

slices.title: Implement Campaign Engine and Spintax Parser
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want to build the CSV importer and spintax message generator, so that campaigns can execute automatically.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a CSV file, When uploaded, Then leads are parsed and stored in the database.
Given a spintax template, When a message is generated, Then a randomized variation is produced.
Given campaign limits, When an account reaches its limit, Then the system rotates to the next available account.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Message generation and account rotation logic
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% uniqueness of generated spintax messages per lead

slices.title: Implement Campaign Management Frontend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want to build Svelte interfaces for campaign creation and CSV upload, so that administrators can launch outreach.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the Svelte application, When an admin creates a campaign, Then they can input a spintax template.
Given a lead list, When an admin uploads a CSV, Then the UI shows parsing progress and results.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce time to launch a campaign to <3 minutes

epics.title: AI Dialog Engine
epics.jtbd: When a prospect replies, I want an AI agent to automatically qualify the lead using custom prompts, so that I can generate meetings without manual chat processing.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase automated lead qualification rate to >40%

slices.title: Define Dialog and Context Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to define the schema for dialog history and AI prompts, so that the LLM has context to generate accurate responses.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given PostgreSQL is used, When defining the schema, Then tables for dialog history and AI configurations are created.
Given an active chat, When messages are exchanged, Then they are stored chronologically.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% of incoming messages are persisted within 100ms

slices.title: Implement Telegram Event Listener
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want to build the incoming message listener and trigger logic, so that the system detects prospect replies.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active session, When a prospect replies, Then the system intercepts the message and updates dialog status.
Given stop-action rules, When negative intents or limits are reached, Then the AI is paused and marked for human intervention.

slices.title: Implement Spring AI Integration
slices.jtbd: When implementing the LLM engine for this epic, I want to integrate Spring AI, so that prospect replies are answered automatically based on history and prompts.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a new incoming message, When the AI is active, Then a response is generated using the predefined system prompt and context.
Given LLM generation, When an API error occurs, Then the system retries or gracefully marks the chat for human intervention.
slices.tocConstraintRef: LLM API latency and context window limits
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure AI response generation time remains under 3 seconds

epics.title: Operator Live Chat CRM
epics.jtbd: When an AI conversation needs intervention, I want a unified chat interface with status tagging, so that an operator can manually take over and close the deal.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease operator response time to escalated chats to <5 minutes

slices.title: Define CRM Status Schema
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to define the schema for dialog statuses and operator assignments, so that the CRM can filter chats.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given PostgreSQL is used, When defining the schema, Then fields for chat status and assigned operator are added to the dialog table.
Given an operator taking over, When recorded, Then the AI status is disabled.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% integrity of operator assignment data

slices.title: Define Live Chat API Contract
slices.jtbd: When implementing the shared API for this epic, I want to define endpoints and websockets for live messaging, so that frontend and backend can be built in parallel.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the need for real-time updates, When defining the API, Then websocket/SSE channels for new messages are documented.
Given the need for manual override, When defining the API, Then endpoints for sending manual messages and changing statuses are documented.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure 100% of real-time events are covered in the API contract

slices.title: Implement Live Chat Backend and Overrides
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want to build real-time event streaming and manual override logic, so that operators can seamlessly take control.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a chat escalated to an operator, When the operator sends a message, Then the AI auto-reply is permanently disabled for that chat.
Given a new message from a prospect, When it arrives, Then it is broadcast to connected Svelte clients via websocket/SSE.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Ensure websocket message delivery latency is <100ms

slices.title: Implement Svelte Live Chat CRM Interface
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want to build a unified inbox UI with color-coded statuses, so that operators can efficiently manage escalated chats.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the live chat UI, When an operator views the inbox, Then dialogs are color-coded based on AI/Human status.
Given a specific dialog, When the operator types and sends a message, Then the message is dispatched and the chat status updates to 'Human'.
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce UI rendering time for large chat histories to <200ms
